title: Spotify engineering culture
subtitle: Squads, tribes, chapters, and guilds for autonomous teams
source_label: Spotify engineering blog, Kniberg squad whitepaper
context: Spotify grew fast. They documented an org model where small squads own features end to end, tribes align squads on a mission, chapters share craft skills, and guilds spread knowledge. Widely cited as a reference for agile at scale in software companies.
before_summary: Growing pains, coordination overhead, releases that did not match learning speed.
after_summary: Autonomous squads with alignment through tribes and light weight governance.
changes: Squads as mini startup units with PO and tech lead
Tribes group squads on related product missions
Chapters for craft standards across squads
Loosely coupled architecture to reduce handoffs

sprints.goal: Define squad boundaries for playlist experience
Ship experiment toggle for recommendation squad
sprints.notes: guild review on API conventions helped
squad autonomy improved after architecture talk

impediments.title: Shared mobile client bottleneck
impediments.notes: two squads needed same release train slot

impediments.title: Experiment platform quota limit
impediments.notes: blocked A/B test for a week

retros.action: Flag cross squad dependencies in tribe sync not only retro
glad: Squad could ship a thin vertical slice
sad: Chapter review came too late in sprint
mad: Duplicate meetings with tribe and chapter

retros.action: Guild office hours for API design questions
mad: Release train stress at sprint end
